2017-03-25.abstract: A growing ferment on Twitter about the new restaurant at Montreal’s government-owned casino, and an end to a life-size cardboard cutout.

2017-03-25.title: U.N. Takes Step Toward Future Prosecutions Against North Korea
2017-03-25.abstract: Under a resolution adopted by the Human Rights Council, criminal justice experts can devise prosecution strategies for pursuing North Korean officials and institutions.

2017-03-25.title: Alone in the Wild for a Year, TV Contestants Learn Their Show Was Canceled
2017-03-25.abstract: Entrants in the reality television series “Eden” emerged from the Scottish wilderness, only to learn the show stopped broadcasting last summer.

2017-03-25.title: Canadians Adopted Refugee Families for a Year. Then Came ‘Month 13.’
2017-03-25.abstract: Everyday Canadians spent a year embracing Syrians in the world’s most personal resettlement program. Letting them go might be the biggest test yet.

2017-03-25.title: Missing Taiwanese Man May Be in Chinese Custody, Some Fear
2017-03-25.abstract: Lee Ming-cheh, who has a background in human rights activism, has not been heard from since Sunday, when he boarded a flight from Taipei to Macau, friends and relatives say.

2017-03-26.title: Drone Strike Kills Planner of 2008 Islamabad Hotel Bombing, U.S. Says
2017-03-26.abstract: The man who was killed, Qari Yasin, had ties to the Pakistani Taliban. The 2008 bombing killed more than 50 people in Pakistan’s capital.

2017-03-26.title: Carrie Lam Wins Vote to Become Hong Kong’s Next Leader
2017-03-26.abstract: Mrs. Lam, Beijing’s preferred candidate in the race, won 777 out of 1,163 votes cast. She defeated John Tsang, a former finance secretary who polls indicated was more popular with the public.

2017-03-26.title: China Bars Professor at Australian University From Leaving, Lawyer Says
2017-03-26.abstract: Feng Chongyi, an Australian permanent resident who has criticized Beijing’s clampdown on dissent, was being questioned in Guangzhou as a suspected national security threat, his lawyer said.

2017-03-26.title: When a President Says, ‘I’ll Kill You’
2017-03-26.abstract: On the streets of Manila, the body count rises daily as journalists document the crusade of President Rodrigo Duterte.

2017-03-26.title: Angela Merkel’s Re-election Bid Is Buoyed by Widely Watched State Election
2017-03-26.abstract: Results from Sunday’s vote in tiny Saarland gave the German chancellor’s Christian Democrats about 40 percent of the vote, a boost for Ms. Merkel as she seeks her fourth term as chancellor.

2017-03-27.title: Conservative Protesters in Brazil Tilt Further Right as Turnout Thins
2017-03-27.abstract: The number of protesters at demonstrations has dwindled since the impeachment of President Dilma Rousseff, but their demands have hardened.

2017-03-27.title: Bulgaria’s Ex-Premier Nears Return to Power in a Key Election for Europe
2017-03-27.abstract: The result was seen as a sign that Bulgarians still see their future lying more with the European Union and less with Russia.

2017-03-27.title: ‘Why Do All the Jihadis Come to Birmingham?’
2017-03-27.abstract: Birmingham, Britain’s second-largest city, with a large Muslim population, has become known as a recruiting ground. Muslim residents resent the stigma but acknowledge the problem.

2017-03-27.title: South Korean Prosecutors Seek to Arrest Park Geun-hye
2017-03-27.abstract: Prosecutors requested a warrant for Ms. Park, who was removed from office as South Korea’s president this month, on charges of bribery and abuse of power.

2017-03-27.title: Cyclone Debbie Strikes Queensland in Australia With Full Fury
2017-03-27.abstract: The storm, with wind as high as 160 miles per hour, forced thousands to leave their homes, halted ferry and train service and closed an airport.

2017-03-28.title: In Protests, Kremlin Fears a Young Generation Stirring
2017-03-28.abstract: The youthfulness of the anticorruption protesters across Russia this weekend surprised even organizers, and clearly rattled the government of Vladimir V. Putin.

2017-03-28.title: Netanyahu and Trump Skip Aipac Meeting
2017-03-28.abstract: The absence of the president and Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu of Israel at the high-profile conference spoke to the fluidity of the nations’ relationship.

2017-03-28.title: Cyclone Debbie Strikes Australia’s Coastline as Thousands Flee
2017-03-28.abstract: Emergency responders struggled to assess the full force of the storm after the loss of telephone service and power in at least 48,000 homes.

2017-03-28.title: Australian Vote on Extradition Treaty With China Is Canceled
2017-03-28.abstract: The canceling of the vote by Prime Minister Malcolm Turnbull signaled that concerns over China’s human rights record would continue to limit the two countries’ ties.

2017-03-28.title: On State TV, Chinese Forces Storm a Compound a Lot Like Bin Laden’s
2017-03-28.abstract: A news broadcast showed the police training at a replica of the house where Osama bin Laden was killed in 2011. It took an eagle-eyed viewer to point it out.

2017-03-29.title: F.B.I. Arrests Iraqi Refugees Related to American’s Kidnapper
2017-03-29.abstract: The two refugees in Virginia are accused of failing to disclose that a brother had been involved in the 2004 kidnapping of an American contractor in Iraq.

2017-03-29.title: Carlos the Jackal Receives a Third Life Sentence in France
2017-03-29.abstract: The terrorist, Ilich Ramírez Sánchez, was convicted of tossing a hand grenade into a Paris drugstore in 1974, killing two and wounding 34.

2017-03-29.title: China Poised to Take Lead on Climate After Trump’s Move to Undo Policies
2017-03-29.abstract: For years, the United States pushed China to commit to limiting its use of fossil fuels. The countries now look likely to switch roles.

2017-03-29.title: China Says It Has Detained Rights Activist From Taiwan
2017-03-29.abstract: The detention adds to signs of an intensified clampdown on outsiders working with China’s beleaguered rights lawyers and groups.

2017-03-29.title: U.K. Initiates ‘Brexit’ and Wades Into a Thorny Thicket
2017-03-29.abstract: Prime Minister Theresa May gave notice of Britain’s withdrawal from the European Union, kicking off a two-year negotiation steeped in peril for both sides.

2017-03-30.title: Meeting in Turkey With Erdogan May Be Tillerson’s Toughest
2017-03-30.abstract: Secretary of State Rex W. Tillerson faces difficult talks over a Turkish constitutional referendum, the fight against ISIS, and Turkish demands for an extradition.

2017-03-30.title: U.S. War Footprint Grows in Middle East, With No Endgame in Sight
2017-03-30.abstract: Two months after President Trump took office, indications are mounting that the military is deepening its involvement in complex wars that lack clear outcomes.

2017-03-30.title: El Salvador, Prizing Water Over Gold, Bans All Metal Mining
2017-03-30.abstract: Environmental activists said the country was the first in the world to impose a nationwide ban on the mining of gold and other metals.

2017-03-30.title: The Road to Nowhere
2017-03-30.abstract: People seeking safety from Boko Haram — many of them women and children — are living along a highway in Niger, struggling to survive.

2017-03-30.title: Fleeing Boko Haram, Thousands Cling to a Road to Nowhere
2017-03-30.abstract: Times journalists spent weeks documenting the stories of people living along a desert highway in Niger, interviewing more than 100 residents scattered by Boko Haram.

2017-03-31.title: Eduardo Cunha, Who Led Impeachment Drive Against Rival in Brazil, Gets a 15-Year Jail Term
2017-03-31.abstract: Mr. Cunha, the politician who led the ouster of Dilma Rousseff, was found guilty of corruption, money laundering and illegally sending money abroad.

2017-03-31.title: In Anatomy of an Afghan Bombing, Clues of a Tangled War
2017-03-31.abstract: Suspicions run high after explosions in a secure guesthouse almost devastated the Kandahar leadership and killed six Arab diplomats.

2017-03-31.title: Turkey’s Wars: Quelling Kurdish Uprisings, and Suffering From Terror Attacks
2017-03-31.abstract: What happens to a country battling ISIS and an insurgency? In Turkey, it means thousands of traumatized Kurdish families, and young people afraid to go out at night.

2017-03-31.title: India, Long at Odds With Pakistan, May Be Rethinking Nuclear First Strikes
2017-03-31.abstract: Indian leaders are thought to be pondering pre-emptive strikes against the Pakistani arsenal in the event of war. That could alter a tenuous balance of power.

2017-03-31.title: A Chinese-Backed Dam Project Leaves Myanmar in a Bind
2017-03-31.abstract: The unpopular dam poses a daunting test for Myanmar’s leader, Daw Aung San Suu Kyi: Going ahead with it would anger the public. Killing it would anger China.
